titleStmt.title: Letter from Stopford & Turner to T.Greany, 5 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Stopford & Turner

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
This is a letter to Mr Greany of the Estate offices of the Brantry Estate in Muckross, Killarney. This letter concerns a pension cheque for an employee named Murhill.
This letter is from the extensive Bantry Estate Collection. It covers a period of 300 years, and contains the formal records regarding the legal, financial and general administration of this large house and estate in County Cork.

msIdentifier.repository: UCC Library Special Collections and Archives

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
5th July 1916. T. Greany Esq.,
Estate Office,
Muckross,
Killarney. Dear Mr Greany, Murhill's Pension Enclosed we send you Mr Lecky's cheque
our favour for £3. 18. 0 endorsed payable
to you being in discharge of Murhill's pensio
for quarter ended 24th June last will you
please let us have his receipt. Yours very truly, (SGD) STOPFORD & TURNER.
J.A.
